file_name: ClickstreamCorp_20200330_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_12089935_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Development Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: The Developer agrees to do each of the following: 1. Create the Application System as detailed in Exhibit A to this Agreement and extend its best efforts to ensure that the design and functionality of the Application System meets the Client's specifications.
perturbation.changed_text: The Developer agrees to do each of the following: 1. Create the App as detailed in Exhibit A to this Agreement and extend its best efforts to ensure that the design and functionality of the Application System meets the Client's specifications.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Application System' is replaced with 'App', without defining if they both represent the same thing. This change introduces ambiguity as the App might be interpreted as a component, not necessarily the entire system. This impacts the responsibilities of the developer throughout the document regarding the scope of work.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. On completion of the Application System, assist the Client in installation of the Application System to its final location, which assistance will include helping the Client with its upload of the finished files to the Client's selected Web-hosting Client and submitting for approval on the Apple Store and Google Play Store.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'Application System', and the contradicted text also refers to the 'Application System'. The changed text replaces 'Application System' with 'App' in the initial description of the developer's duties, while the contradicted text still refers to the completion of the 'Application System'. Since the explanation indicates that this introduces ambiguity as to whether the 'App' and 'Application System' are the same thing and impacts the scope of the developer's responsibilities, there is a contradiction. The developer could argue that they are only responsible for completing the 'App', and not the 'Application System' referred to in the contradicted section, leading to uncertainty in the contract's enforceability, fitting the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: The Developer hereby warrants and represents that following delivery of the Application System to the Client (which shall be deeded to occur only on the date the Web Application is uploaded to the AWS for distribution) pursuant to Exhibit A (the "Support Period"), the Application will be free from programming errors and defects in workmanship and materials, and will conform to the specifications of Exhibit A.
perturbation.changed_text: The Developer hereby warrants and represents that following delivery of the App to the Client (which shall be deeded to occur only on the date the Web Application is uploaded to the AWS for distribution) pursuant to Exhibit A (the "Support Period"), the Software will be free from programming errors and defects in workmanship and materials, and will conform to the specifications of Exhibit A.
perturbation.explanation: Here we are changing the terms 'Application System' for 'App' and 'Application' for 'Software'. This perturbation makes it unclear whether 'App' and 'Software' refer to the same deliverable, or whether the support period applies only to the 'Software' and not to other components that are included in the Application. Making the parties unsure if the 'Software' has programming errors and need support after the delivery.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Performance. The Developer hereby warrants and represents that following delivery of the App to the Client (which shall be deeded to occur only on the date the Web Application is uploaded to the AWS for distribution) pursuant to Exhibit A (the "Support Period"), the Software will be free from programming errors and defects in workmanship and materials, and will conform to the specifications of Exhibit A. If programming errors or other defects are discovered during the Support Period, the Developer shall promptly remedy those errors or defects at its own expense. The developer will fix any bugs that may come up from the original contract after the 90 days warranty has passed.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Application System' to 'App' and 'Application' to 'Software' introduces ambiguity regarding the scope of the warranty and support period. The contradicted text repeats the changed terms, but the initial modification creates uncertainty about whether 'App' and 'Software' encompass all components of the original 'Application System'. This uncertainty, as outlined in the explanation, directly impacts the enforceability of the warranty and support obligations, creating a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, because the terms are used interchangeably without a clear definition, leading to conflicting obligations. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition, as it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Developer agrees, during the Term and thereafter, to hold in strictest confidence, and not to use, except for the benefit of the Client, or to disclose to any person, firm, or corporation without the prior written authorization of the Client, any Confidential Information of the Client. "Confidential Information" means any of the Client's proprietary information, technical data, trade secrets, or know-how, including, but not limited to, business plans, research, product plans, products, services, customer lists, markets, software, developments, inventions, processes, formulas, technology, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, or other business information disclosed to the Developer by the Client either directly or indirectly.
perturbation.changed_text: The Developer agrees, during the Term and thereafter, to hold in strictest confidence, and not to use, except for the benefit of the Client, or to disclose to any person, firm, or corporation without the prior written authorization of the Client, any sensitive data of the Client. "Confidential Information" means any of the Client's proprietary information, technical data, trade secrets, or know-how, including, but not limited to, business plans, research, product plans, products, services, customer lists, markets, software, developments, inventions, processes, formulas, technology, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, or other business information disclosed to the Developer by the Client either directly or indirectly.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'sensitive data' in the first sentence. However, the definition that follows still defines 'Confidential Information' and what it encompases. This creates a contradiction because the Developer is now obligated to protect 'sensitive data', but the contract only defines 'Confidential Information'. It introduces uncertainty about the scope of the confidentiality obligation: Does it extend only to the items listed in the definition of 'Confidential Information', or does it also include other types of 'sensitive data' not explicitly listed?
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Developer agrees, during the Term and thereafter, to hold in strictest confidence, and not to use, except for the benefit of the Client, or to disclose to any person, firm, or corporation without the prior written authorization of the Client, any sensitive data of the Client. "Confidential Information" means any of the Client's proprietary information, technical data, trade secrets, or know-how, including, but not limited to, business plans, research, product plans, products, services, customer lists, markets, software, developments, inventions, processes, formulas, technology, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, or other business information disclosed to the Developer by the Client either directly or indirectly.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'sensitive data' in the first sentence of the section. However, the subsequent definition still defines 'Confidential Information', creating a direct conflict. The contradiction arises because the developer is obligated to protect 'sensitive data', but the agreement only defines 'Confidential Information'. This creates an ambiguity about what constitutes 'sensitive data' that is not explicitly mentioned in the definition section, leading to in-text contradiction as defined by Misaligned Terminology.
